headline: Foreign Worker Levy Overhaul Raises RM4.2B But Costs Soar
context: The restructured foreign worker levy system introduced in mid-2025 raised RM4.2 billion in revenue but pushed hiring costs 35% higher, leading to worker shortages in plantations, construction, and manufacturing.

cards.big: Foreign worker levies jumped 35% — plantations report 28% labour shortage while 2.4 million permits are active
cards.sub: Higher levies were meant to encourage automation but 82% of plantation work cannot be mechanised currently

cards.big: 2.4 million documented foreign workers contribute estimated RM78 billion to GDP — 4.8% of total output
cards.sub: Levy revenue of RM4.2 billion is less than 6% of foreign workers' economic contribution

cards.big: Undocumented workers estimated at 1.5-2 million pay zero levies and depress wages for Malaysian B40 workers
cards.sub: Construction sector reports 45% of workforce is undocumented — formal levy policy misses nearly half the workers

cards.big: Immigration Department processed levy payments from only 68% of registered employers in 2025
cards.sub: RM1.4 billion in levy arrears uncollected while enforcement capacity covers only 15% of worksites annually

cards.big: Raising levies on legal workers while 2 million illegal workers pay nothing doesn't fix the system — it punishes compliance
Should Malaysia create a single foreign worker management agency combining Immigration, Labour, and levy collection?
